text.title: Mobile Performance Budget Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for setting and enforcing a mobile perf budget.

rule.statement: Budget MUST be defined per route (or route group), NOT one global budget. Routes with different content have different limits.
rule.rationale: A product landing page has different budget than a checkout funnel; one global budget either over-restricts marketing pages or under-restricts checkout.

rule.statement: Budget MUST include LCP (<2.5s), INP (<200ms), CLS (<0.1) thresholds. Bundle-size-only budgets are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bundle size is a proxy; CWVs are what Google ranks and what users feel.

rule.statement: Budget MUST be enforced in CI — Lighthouse CI / bundle-size action / WebPageTest API. A PR that exceeds budget MUST fail the build.
rule.rationale: Documented budgets without CI enforcement decay within 3 months.

rule.statement: Budget validation MUST run on mobile-throttled config (Slow 4G + 4x CPU slowdown OR Lighthouse's default mobile preset). Desktop-only validation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Desktop metrics are not predictive of mobile experience; mobile is where the budget matters.

rule.statement: Budget MUST be reviewed quarterly; changes require a written rationale (e.g. 'added video player feature → LCP budget raised 200ms').
rule.rationale: Drift happens — explicit rationale forces conscious tradeoff vs silent regression.
